---
title: "Classification Summative MISCADA"
output: html_document
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = TRUE)
```

## R Markdown

This is an R Markdown document. Markdown is a simple formatting syntax for authoring HTML, PDF, and MS Word documents. For more details on using R Markdown see <http://rmarkdown.rstudio.com>.

When you click the **Knit** button a document will be generated that includes both content as well as the output of any embedded R code chunks within the document. You can embed an R code chunk like this:

```{r}
#using the heart failure data
heart.data <- readr::read_csv("C:/Users/RNVD/Downloads/heart_failure.csv")
View(heart.data)
dim(heart.data)
skim(heart.data)

```
```{r}
library("skimr")
skim(heart.data)

library("tidyverse")
library("ggplot2")

table(heart.data$fatal_mi)
#there might be class imablance as there are twice as more negative 0 cases as there are positive 1 cases

ggplot(heart.data,
      aes(x = heart.data$age)) +
      geom_histogram()

ggplot(heart.data, aes(x = factor(heart.data$fatal_mi), y = heart.data$ejection_fraction)) +
  geom_boxplot()

#ggplot(heart.data,aes(x=heart.data$anaemia, y=heart.data$fatal_mi))+geom_col()

ggplot(heart.data, aes(x = heart.data$serum_creatinine, fill = factor(heart.data$fatal_mi))) +
  geom_histogram(position = "identity", alpha = 0.5)

ggplot(heart.data, aes(x = heart.data$diabetes, fill = factor(heart.data$fatal_mi))) +
  geom_bar(position = "identity", alpha = 0.5)

ggplot(heart.data, aes(x = factor(heart.data$high_blood_pressure), fill = factor(heart.data$fatal_mi))) +
  geom_bar(position = "dodge")
#these are plots only for the count of smokers and non smokers
# ggplot(heart.data, aes(x = factor(smoking))) +
#   geom_bar() +
#   labs(x = "Smoking (0=No, 1=Yes)", y = "Count")
# 
# ggplot(heart.data, aes(x = factor(diabetes))) +
#   geom_bar() +
#   labs(x = "Diabetes (0=No, 1=Yes)", y = "Count")


#doubtful on these as they are giving similar results
ggplot(heart.data, aes(x = factor(heart.data$smoking), fill = factor(heart.data$fatal_mi))) +
  geom_bar(position = "fill") +
  labs(y = "Proportion")

ggplot(heart.data, aes(x = factor(heart.data$diabetes), fill = factor(heart.data$fatal_mi))) +
  geom_bar(position = "fill")

```


```{r}
#Model Fitting
#tried glm dk wt it does but check
heart_fit <- glm(fatal_mi ~ ., binomial, heart.data)
heart_fit
plot(heart_fit)
summary(heart_fit)
```
```{r}
library("mlr3")
set.seed(200)
heart.data$fatal_mi <- as.factor(heart.data$fatal_mi)

heart.mlr <- TaskClassif$new(id = "heart",
                               backend = heart.data,
                               target = "fatal_mi",
                               positive = "1")
heart.mlr
as.data.table(mlr_learners)

library("mlr3verse")

cv5 <- rsmp("cv", folds = 5)
cv5$instantiate(heart.mlr)

plot(heart.mlr)

```
```{r}
#Model Improvements
lrn_lda <- lrn("classif.lda",predict_type = "prob")
lrn_log <- lrn("classif.log_reg",predict_type = "prob")
lrn_glm <-  lrn("classif.glmnet",predict_type = "prob")
lrn_xgboost <- lrn("classif.xgboost", predict_type = "prob") #might need package
lrn_baseline <- lrn("classif.featureless", predict_type = "prob")
lrn_cart <- lrn("classif.rpart", predict_type = "prob")
lrn_ranger   <- lrn("classif.ranger", predict_type = "prob") #needs package

#Benchmark
res <- benchmark(data.table(
  task=list(heart.mlr),
  learner = list(lrn_lda, lrn_log, lrn_glm,lrn_xgboost, lrn_baseline, lrn_cart,lrn_ranger ),
  resampling = list(cv5)
),store_models = TRUE )
metrics <- as.data.table(res$aggregate(list(
  msr("classif.acc"),
  msr("classif.auc"),
  msr("classif.fpr"),
  msr("classif.fnr")
)))
metrics
autoplot(res)

metrics$learner_id <- factor(metrics$learner_id,
                             levels = c("classif.lda","classif.log_reg","classif.glmnet",
                                        "classif.xgboost","classif.featureless",
                                        "classif.rpart","classif.ranger"),
                             labels = c("LDA","LogReg","glmnet","XGBoost","Baseline","CART","Ranger"))

p1<-ggplot(metrics,
       aes(x = learner_id, y= classif.acc))+
  geom_bar(stat = "identity")+
  scale_x_discrete(guide = guide_axis(n.dodge=2))+
  labs(x="Learner", y="Accuracy")

p2<-ggplot(metrics,
       aes(x = learner_id, y= classif.auc))+
  geom_bar(stat = "identity")+
  scale_x_discrete(guide = guide_axis(n.dodge=2))+
  labs(x="Learner", y="AUC")

p3<-ggplot(metrics,
       aes(x = learner_id, y= classif.fpr))+
  geom_bar(stat = "identity")+
  scale_x_discrete(guide = guide_axis(n.dodge=2))+
  labs(x="Learner", y="False Positive Rate")

p4<-ggplot(metrics,
       aes(x = learner_id, y= classif.fnr))+
  geom_bar(stat = "identity")+
  scale_x_discrete(guide = guide_axis(n.dodge=2))+
  labs(x="Learner", y="False Negative Rate")

gridExtra::grid.arrange(p1, p2, p3, p4, nrow = 2, ncol = 2)

```
```{r}
tree_rpart <- res$resample_result(6)$learners[[1]]$model

# Plot
plot(tree_rpart, compress = TRUE, margin =0.1, uniform=TRUE)
text(tree_rpart, use.n = TRUE, cex=0.8, all=TRUE, xpd = TRUE)
```
```{r}
#dk if this part is necessary - mostly not needed
# Pipeline for preprocessing
pl_missing <- NULL 
pl_factor <- po("encode")  
pl_xgb <- pl_factor %>>% lrn("classif.xgboost", predict_type = "prob")


# Apply to logistic regression
pl_log <- pl_factor %>>% po(lrn("classif.log_reg", predict_type = "prob"))

# Apply to random forest
pl_rf <- pl_factor %>>% po(lrn("classif.ranger", predict_type = "prob"))

# Benchmark again
res2 <- benchmark(data.table(
  task = list(heart.mlr),
  learner = list(pl_log, pl_rf,pl_xgb),
  resampling = list(cv5)
))

as.data.table(res2$aggregate(list(
  msr("classif.acc"),
  msr("classif.auc")
)))

```

```{r}
#hyperparameter tuning
#using random forests and xgboost with diff parameters
set.seed(200)
learners1 <- list(
  lrn("classif.ranger", num.trees = 300,predict_type = "prob", id="numTrees_300"),
  lrn("classif.ranger", num.trees = 500,predict_type = "prob",id="numTrees_500"),
  lrn("classif.ranger", num.trees = 700,predict_type = "prob",id="numTrees_500")
)

res3 <- benchmark(data.table(
  task = list(heart.mlr),
  learner = learners1,
  resampling = list(cv5)
))

metrics3 <- as.data.table(res3$aggregate(list(
  msr("classif.acc"),
  msr("classif.auc"),
  msr("classif.fpr"),
  msr("classif.fnr")
)))
metrics3
autoplot(res3)

```
```{r}
#Final Model
final_model <- lrn("classif.ranger",num_trees=500, predict_type = "prob")

set.seed(200)

split <- partition(heart.mlr, ratio = 0.8)
train_set <- heart.mlr$clone()$filter(split$train)
test_set  <- heart.mlr$clone()$filter(split$test)

final_model$train(train_set)

# Predictions
pred <- final_model$predict(test_set)

pred$score(msr("classif.acc"))
pred$score(msr("classif.auc"))

pred$confusion
```
```{r}
# Change threshold
pred.data.response <- ifelse(pred$prob[,2] > 0.3, 1, 0)

# New confusion matrix
table(truth = pred$truth, pred = pred$data$response)
```
